title: Uniform sum-product phenomenon for algebraic groups and Bremner's conjecture

thm:1.1.summary: Bremner's conjecture resolved: For elliptic curve $E$ in Weierstrass form with rank $r$, any arithmetic progression, geometric progression, or squared-AP in coordinates has size at most $C^{1+r}$.

conjecture:1.2.summary: Bremner's original conjecture on bounded arithmetic progressions in elliptic curve coordinates.

thm:1.3.summary: Sum-product for algebraic groups: For 1-dim algebraic group $G \ne G_a$, finite set $A$, correspondences $C_i$, either sumset or correspondences-sum grows polynomially.

cor:1.4.summary: Corollary of the uniform sum-product phenomenon.

thm:1.5.summary: Elekes-Szabó for small doubling: For $A$ with $|A+A| \le K|A|$ in algebraic group $G \ne G_a$, variety $V$ intersects $A^g$ in at most $K^C + |A|^{\dim(V)-1}$ points.

thm:1.8.summary: Non-degenerate projection bound: For non-degenerate variety $V$ and set $A$ with small doubling, projection of $V \cap A^g$ has size at least $|A|^g / K^{O(1)}$.

thm:2.1.summary: Generalized Bremner: Bounds APs/GPs in coordinates of arbitrary algebraic groups.

thm:2.3.summary: Bourgain-Chang type sum-product: For correspondence $C$ of degree $d$, either $|A+A| > |A|^{1+\delta}$ or $|C(A)+C(A)| \ge |A|^{2-D\delta}$.

thm:2.6.summary: Elekes-Szabó with codeficiency: Variety $V$ intersects $A^g$ in at most $K^C + |A|^{\mathrm{codef}(V)}$ points.

lem:6.1.summary: Freiman-type rank reduction: If $|nA| \le K|A|$, then subset $A'$ has bounded rank $d \ll \log(K)/\log(n)$.

lem:6.2.summary: Doubling to rank: If $|A+A| \le K|A|$, then subset $A'$ of size $\ge |A|/K^{C'}$ has rank $\le C \cdot \log(K)$. Uses weak PFR over $\mathbb{Z}$.

lem:6.3.summary: $\mathbb{Z}^D$ version: Small doubling in $\mathbb{Z}^D$ implies subset lies in low-dimensional sublattice. Direct application of GGMT weak PFR.

thm:4.2.summary: Cartesian product projection theorem: Key geometric tool for bounding projections.

prop:5.1.summary: Non-degenerate correspondences and cosets: Structural result on correspondence behavior.

thm:A.1.summary: Diophantine equations toolbox from David-Philippon, Laurent, Evertse-Schmidt-Schlickewei.

edges.note: The $\mathbb{Z}^D$ rank lemma yields the algebraic-group doubling-to-rank lemma.
The Elekes-Szabó bound for small doubling uses the doubling-to-rank reduction.
The codeficiency bound uses the doubling-to-rank reduction.
The non-degenerate projection bound uses the Cartesian-product projection theorem.
The sum-product dichotomy rests on the non-degenerate projection bound.
The sum-product phenomenon for algebraic groups settles Bremner's conjecture.
The sum-product dichotomy uses the Bourgain-Chang type estimate.
The non-degenerate projection bound uses the coset structure of non-degenerate correspondences.
The coset-structure result uses the Diophantine toolbox of Appendix A.
The codeficiency version generalizes the dimension-based Elekes-Szabó bound.
The doubling-to-rank lemma uses the $n$-fold sumset rank-reduction lemma.
